frameworks.name: NeuroAI
frameworks.one_line: biologically grounded AI research program inspired by animal sensorimotor competence

frameworks.name: embodied Turing test
frameworks.one_line: benchmark requiring AI animal models to match living counterparts in sensorimotor interaction

core_question: What research agenda will catalyze the next generation of AI by drawing on neuroscience and evolved animal capabilities?
key_assumptions: neuroscience has historically driven AI progress
capabilities shared across animals reflect 500+ million years of evolution
embodied sensorimotor competence is a meaningful AI benchmark
manipulations: proposed shift of evaluation target from human-unique skills (language, games) to animal-shared sensorimotor skills
key_findings: argues fundamental NeuroAI investment is needed to accelerate AI progress
introduces the embodied Turing test as a core evaluative challenge
interpretations: building models that pass the embodied Turing test would provide a roadmap for next-generation AI
